haven_overview.concept: The Haven is NCL's 'ship within a ship' — a private, luxury complex occupying decks 17 and 18 of the Norwegian Luna. Haven guests have exclusive access to a private pool deck, restaurant, bar, lounge, and concierge. It's quieter, more attentive, and insulated from the main ship crowd. The experience is fundamentally different from a standard NCL cruise.
key_areas: Haven Pool Deck — private, adults-preferred, rarely crowded
Haven Restaurant — private dining room, breakfast and dinner daily
Haven Lounge & Bar — private bar, cocktail hours, evening social space
Haven Concierge Desk — your single point of contact for everything
Haven Sundeck — upper deck above the pool, panoramic views
haven_overview.access: Haven key card required for elevator and keypad access to Haven areas. Do not lose your key card.

tips.detail: Haven guests have a dedicated embarkation line. Look for Haven/Suite signage at the port. You should be onboard within 30-45 minutes of arriving — no general boarding lines.

tips.tip: Go straight to the Haven on embarkation
tips.detail: Once onboard, go directly to the Haven (decks 17/18). Your butler will meet you or be assigned quickly. The Haven Restaurant is open for lunch on embarkation day — avoid the chaotic Garden Café crowds.

Introduce yourself and Holly to your butler. Mention your preferences: anniversary trip, celiac allergy, suite decoration request, any drink preferences you want stocked, pillow preference, and daily schedule. The butler is your personal concierge for everything inside the suite.

tips.tip: Visit the Haven Concierge desk on Day 1
tips.detail: Make all your requests here: anniversary suite decoration, GF allergy note fleet-wide, specialty restaurant reservations, show priority seating (especially Rocket Man on April 5), and any excursion questions. The concierge desk is most accessible right at embarkation before the rush.

tips.tip: Book specialty restaurants on embarkation day
tips.detail: Onda, Cagney's, Hasuki, Le Bistro, and Los Lobos fill up fast. Book your preferred evenings at the specialty dining desk or through the concierge on Day 1. Haven guests sometimes get pre-allocated reservations — ask the concierge if any are pre-booked.
Stop by the spa desk (or have the concierge confirm) that the April 5 treatments — Ultimate Treatment Facial and Fire & Ice Mani/Pedi — are confirmed with exact times.

This is not an exaggeration. Your butler is available 24/7 for: unpacking/packing luggage, ordering room service (including specialty restaurant meals delivered to your suite), stocking your mini-bar, making reservations, arranging surprises, running errands on the ship, and anything else you can think of. If you're unsure whether you can ask — ask anyway.

Tell the butler: preferred drinks for the mini-bar, whether you want turndown service at a specific time, pillow type (soft/firm/feather/foam), preferred breakfast time and items, and any anniversary touches you haven't already arranged.

For April 5 anniversary dinner: the butler can arrange an Onda at Sea balcony dinner — Onda's menu items delivered to your suite balcony with full table setup. This is one of the most memorable experiences possible. Request this at least a day in advance if not already arranged.

tips.tip: Breakfast in suite vs. Haven Restaurant
tips.detail: Both are excellent. Suite breakfast via butler is private and can be timed exactly. Haven Restaurant breakfast has a fuller menu and a social atmosphere. Alternate throughout the week based on your mood.
Tip the butler on the last evening (April 10). See the tipping guide section for recommended amounts.

tips.tip: Haven pool is adults-preferred and rarely crowded
tips.detail: The Haven pool deck is the biggest differentiator from standard NCL. While the main pool deck is chaotic with chair-saving and crowds, the Haven deck has abundant seating, attentive pool service, and a quiet atmosphere. On port days, you may have it virtually to yourself.

tips.tip: Haven butler serves drinks at pool
tips.detail: You don't need to go to the bar — the Haven pool staff will come to you. Flag them down or order through your butler.

When most guests are off the ship at Harvest Caye, Roatán, Costa Maya, or Cozumel, the Haven deck is exceptionally quiet. Consider staying onboard one port day (Cozumel is the natural choice) to experience the Haven at its least crowded.
Above the pool there's usually a smaller, quieter sundeck with panoramic views. Often overlooked — great for stargazing at night or a quiet sunrise spot.

tips.tip: The concierge can solve almost any problem
tips.detail: Long line at a restaurant? Concierge gets you in. Show is sold out? Concierge holds Haven seats. Want a specific shore excursion added late? Concierge can try. The concierge's job is to make everything seamless — use them freely.
For Rocket Man, Lunatique, and HIKO — request Haven priority seating through the concierge on Day 1 for all shows you want to see. Haven seats are a designated section, not general admission.
Ask the concierge for an overview of the week's entertainment and special events on Day 1. They know what's popular, what fills up, and what's worth seeing.
Breakfast and dinner in the Haven Restaurant are private and excellent. Use specialty restaurants for variety but treat Haven Restaurant as home base. It's always a safe, comfortable option.

Haven Restaurant doesn't serve lunch (typically). Options: Garden Café (Holly should be cautious), Pool Deck Grill, specialty restaurants (check if open for lunch), room service via butler, or port dining.

Target nights for Onda (anniversary balcony dinner already planned), Cagney's for a sea day, and potentially Hasuki or Le Bistro for variety. Haven guests may get priority reservations — confirm with concierge.

tips.tip: The Free at Sea dining package
tips.detail: If you purchased a Free at Sea specialty dining package, each dinner covers a set amount per person. Confirm your package details with the concierge to know how many specialty dinners are pre-paid.

Confirm which bars and venues your drink package covers — Haven bar, specialty restaurants, room service, and pool deck should all be included. Verify with the concierge on Day 1 to avoid surprise charges.
If you have an internet package, the NCL app works ship-to-ship without internet for messaging and viewing daily schedules. Save the paid internet for important communications.

tips.tip: Freestyle Daily — read it every night
tips.detail: The single most important habit for maximizing your cruise. Delivered to the suite each evening. Plan the next day over a nightcap. Takes 5 minutes.
Check your onboard account every 2-3 days via the TV in-room system or the NCL app. Catch any billing errors early — they're much easier to resolve mid-cruise than at debarkation.

NCL now uses digital/in-app muster drill. Complete the video portion on the app, then check in at your assigned muster station. Much quicker than the old all-hands-on-deck drill. Must be done before the ship departs.

tipping_guide.overview: NCL charges a daily service charge (prepaid or automatic) that covers gratuities for most crew: room steward, dining room staff, buffet staff, and other general crew. This is typically $20-25/person/day and may already be prepaid with your booking. Additional tipping for Haven staff is customary and reflects the elevated service level.
prepaid_covers: Main and specialty dining room servers and bussers
Bar staff (15-20% auto-gratuity is usually added to each drink)

additional_tips.when: Last evening of the cruise (April 10 evening)
additional_tips.how: Cash in an envelope. The butler does NOT receive a share of the prepaid gratuity pool — this is entirely separate.
additional_tips.guidance: For exceptional service and heavy butler use (which you should have), aim for $200-250 total. On the high end if he arranged the balcony dinner, anniversary setup, and kept your suite stocked perfectly.

additional_tips.how: Cash in an envelope addressed to the concierge by name
additional_tips.guidance: If the concierge arranged priority embarkation/debarkation, secured Haven show seats, handled GF allergy coordination, and solved problems throughout the week — $75-100 is appropriate.

additional_tips.amount: $1–$2 per drink on top of auto-gratuity
additional_tips.how: Add to the receipt when signing or hand cash directly
additional_tips.guidance: The 18-20% auto-gratuity is added to all drinks. A small additional cash tip for regular bar staff who know your name and your drink is appreciated and ensures continued excellent service.

additional_tips.when: Last evening or leave in envelope in the suite
additional_tips.how: Cash envelope — can reference the steward by name
additional_tips.guidance: Covered by prepaid gratuity but a small extra cash tip acknowledges exceptional work, especially if they accommodated special requests (anniversary setup, specific turndown timing).

additional_tips.when: At the end of the meal
additional_tips.how: Add to the receipt or leave cash on the table
additional_tips.guidance: Specialty restaurants have an additional cover charge already built in, which includes some gratuity. Extra cash is optional but appreciated for standout service, especially if they managed Holly's celiac requirements with care.

additional_tips.when: At the end of the excursion
additional_tips.guidance: Standard for any guided excursion. For the Roatán wildlife sanctuary, $10/person for a good guide is appropriate.

additional_tips.amount: 18% gratuity typically auto-added to spa treatments
additional_tips.how: Check the spa receipt — if not auto-added, 18-20% is standard
additional_tips.guidance: The spa auto-gratuity is standard. Additional cash for an exceptional treatment (especially on the anniversary facial) is a nice gesture but not expected.

cash_preparation.total_estimated: $300–$450 in cash for all tipping across the week
cash_preparation.bills_to_bring: Primarily $1, $5, and $20 bills. A few $50s for butler/concierge envelopes.
cash_preparation.currency: USD is universally accepted and preferred. Do not tip in foreign currency.
cash_preparation.tip: Prepare envelopes on the second-to-last evening (April 9) so you're not scrambling on the last day

casino_guide.overview: Norwegian Luna's casino is a full Las Vegas-style facility with table games, slots, and poker. Open in international waters (closes while in port). The casino is a significant entertainment option for evening hours.

day_one_actions.action: Sign up for Casinos at Sea
day_one_actions.where: Casino Players Club desk (inside the casino)
day_one_actions.when: Day 1 evening, once the casino opens (after leaving Miami port)
day_one_actions.benefit: Earn Tier Credits toward future NCL cruise discounts and free slot play. Higher tiers give better perks.

day_one_actions.where: Same casino desk or via Caesars app
day_one_actions.benefit: Points earned on the ship transfer to Caesars Rewards and are redeemable at Caesars hotels and casinos worldwide. Tier matching sometimes available.

day_one_actions.action: Ask about Players Club free drinks
day_one_actions.benefit: Active table game players typically receive complimentary drinks — confirm the policy with the pit boss or dealer.

games_overview.table_minimums: Typically $10–$25 minimum. Higher on sea days/weekends.
games_overview.house_edge: ~0.5% with basic strategy — lowest house edge of common table games
games_overview.strategy: See entertainment.json for full basic strategy reminders
games_overview.holly_tip: Stick to basic strategy. Do not take insurance. $10 table is fine for casual play.

games_overview.tip: Penny slots have lower denomination but high house edge. Video poker (if available) has much better odds than slots.

games_overview.house_edge: 5.26% American (double zero). 2.7% European (single zero if available)
games_overview.tip: Stick to even-money bets (red/black, odd/even, 1-18/19-36) for the lowest variance. Avoid single-number bets unless you like excitement over odds.

games_overview.variants: Texas Hold'em cash game or tournament. Three Card Poker as a table game.
games_overview.tip: Poker tournaments usually $25-65 entry — fun format, meets other players, capped downside.

approach: Set a hard loss limit per session — walk away when it's gone
A 'winning' session: cash out your original buy-in once you're up 50-100%
Don't chase losses — if you've lost your session budget, come back tomorrow
Blackjack with basic strategy is the best bang for your casino dollar
The casino is entertainment, not income — budget accordingly
best_casino_sessions: April 4 (embarkation evening) — light warm-up session, see the layout
April 5 (anniversary sea day) — after the show, late night
April 10 (final sea day) — last session, use remaining budget

blackjack_tournament.format: Usually 20-30 minute rounds at a dedicated tournament table
blackjack_tournament.prize: Ship credit, future cruise credit, or cash — varies by ship/session
blackjack_tournament.how_to_enter: Sign up at the casino desk — morning of the tournament day
blackjack_tournament.tip: Tournament strategy differs from basic strategy — you're playing against other players' chip counts, not the house. More aggressive play is warranted when behind.

slots_tournament.format: Timed session on designated tournament machines — most credits at the end wins
slots_tournament.how_to_enter: Casino desk — may be free for Casinos at Sea members
slots_tournament.tip: Bet max credits on each spin — only way to maximize score in timed format

packing_tips: Suite 12846 (Haven King H5) has extensive storage — you can unpack fully and live out of the closet/drawers
Ask the butler to unpack your luggage on Day 1 — that's what they're there for
Start packing on the second-to-last evening (April 9) — not the morning of debarkation
Luggage can be left outside your suite door the night before debarkation (April 10 evening) for the crew to collect — leave one bag with overnight essentials
Haven guests disembark first — concierge arranges priority debarkation, typically before general boarding groups
debarkation_tips: Haven priority debarkation — concierge arranges this, confirm on April 10
Haven guests typically depart before 8:30am — earlier than all other groups
Checked luggage is collected by crew the night before and awaits you in the terminal
Carry-on items stay with you overnight and you carry them off
Have transportation arranged in advance — port pickup, rideshare, or hotel shuttle
US Customs form required — complete the night before if paper forms are distributed
NCL app may have digital customs declaration — check before sailing
